This gripping and expertly crafted tale promises an intensely suspenseful and profoundly terrifying experience for devoted fans of both science fiction and the horror genre.

**Additional Works by S.A. Barnes:**
*   Ghost Station
*   Cold Eternity

*Kindly note: This title is being distributed without Digital Rights Management Software (DRM) applied, as per the publisher's directive.*

Name: Nightmare
Description:
Mary Shelley's creation, Frankenstein, who emerged from a dream by a young woman who had just lost her first child, has transformed itself into a warning about the dangers of tampering with nature. The vampire started life as a sexual fantasy, and Bram Stoker's tale became a metaphor for dominance and dependence in sexual relationships. Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde, perhaps the most psychological of all horror stories, examines the beast in man and the dark side of human nature. And The Hound of the Baskervilles is a tale of conflict between rationalism and folklore, and the skills of Sherlock Holmes.

## Dreams: Your Body's Hidden Health Signals

Your dreams are more than just random images; they can be powerful indicators of your physical and emotional well-being, even aiding in your healing. For centuries, people have looked to dreams for guidance, and modern science is now beginning to validate this ancient practice. This exploration reveals how dreams can act as early warning systems for health problems and contribute to your recovery.

Authors Dr. Larry Burk and Kathleen O’Keefe-Kanavos, winners of a 2018 Nautilus Silver Award, have gathered extensive research and true stories highlighting this connection. Their work examines medical studies and investigations into precognitive dreams, showcasing documented cases where dreams accurately identified illnesses before conventional diagnosis, and influenced healing. O’Keefe-Kanavos's own experience as a three-time breast cancer survivor exemplifies this. Her dreams diagnosed her condition when initial medical tests were inconclusive, demonstrating the subconscious mind's ability to guide us toward necessary health actions.

To utilize this resource, learn practical strategies for understanding your dreams. Discover effective dream journaling and interpretation techniques to build trust in your dreams as a source of healing wisdom. By actively engaging with your dreams, you can access their deep inner guidance, empowering you to navigate your health and life with greater awareness and insight.

Name: The Nightmare
Description:
In 'The Nightmare,' Gertrude Mabel Barrows Bennett crafts an unsettling narrative that delves into the realms of psychological horror and the uncanny. Through her innovative use of stream-of-consciousness narration, Bennett immerses readers in the protagonist's fragmented psyche, evoking a pervasive sense of dread and existential despair. The novella, written in the early 20th century, reflects the anxieties of a rapidly modernizing society, grappling with the impact of modernity on individual identity and sanity. Bennett'Äôs penchant for blending exquisite language with haunting imagery fosters an immersive literary experience, making the story not only a compelling read but also a profound exploration of fear and the subconscious. Bennett, often considered a pioneer in women'Äôs speculative fiction, drew from her own experiences and societal tensions in her writing. Born in 1887, she faced the challenges of her time, including the constraints on women's expression in literature. Her distinct voice and visionary perspectives reflect a psychological depth that resonate with the themes prevalent during the early women'Äôs suffrage movement and the burgeoning realm of gothic literature. This evocative and meticulously crafted novella is highly recommended for readers seeking a thought-provoking exploration of fear and identity. 'The Nightmare' not only entertains but also engages in a deeper conversation about the human experience in confronting inner demons. This work is essential for scholars of horror fiction and serves as a significant contribution to the canon of early 20th-century literature.

Name: Alone with the Horrors
Description:
“A seminal collection of one of late 20th century’s most important horror writers . . . every horror fan should have on their bookshelf.” —SF Site Reviews Ramsey Campbell is perhaps the world’s most decorated author of horror fiction. He has won four World Fantasy Awards, ten British Fantasy Awards, three Bram Stoker Awards, and the Horror Writers’ Association’s Lifetime Achievement Award. Three decades into his career, Campbell paused to review his body of short fiction and selected the stories that were, to his mind, the very best of his work. Alone With the Horrors collects nearly forty tales from the first thirty years of Campbell’s writing, including several award winners. Campbell crowns the book with a lengthy preface—revised for this edition—that traces his early publication history, discusses his youthful correspondence with August Derleth, and illuminates the influence of H. P. Lovecraft on his work. Alone with the Horrors provides readers with a close look at a powerful writer’s development of his craft. “The marrow-chilling tales in this comprehensive, chronologically arranged collection, selected from Campbell’s 30-year career, demonstrate the ways this sophisticated British writer inspires fear without resorting to blood and gore.” —Publishers Weekly

Name: Psychological Reflections on Cinematic Terror
Description:
In this examination of the psychology of terror, Iaccino uses Jungian archetypes to analyze significant works in the horror film genre. In the past, Jungian archetypes have been used to interpret mythologies, to examine great works of literature, and to explain why sexual stereotypes persist in our society. Here, for the first time, Iaccino applies such models as the Cursed Wanderers, the Warrior Amazons, the Random Destroyers, and the Techno-Myths to highlight recurrent themes in a wide range of films, from early classics such as Nosferatu to the contemporary Nightmare on Elm Street and Alien series. With this innovative approach, Iaccino gains a new perspective on the psychology of the often powerful compulsion to be scared.

Name: Singing with All My Skin and Bone
Description:
The staggeringly brilliant and astonishing debut collection by powerful stylist Sunny Moraine. A heady stew of dark fantasy, dystopia, terror, and transcendence. "Sex, oddity, horror, transfiguration: Sunny Moraine's stories cut straight through to the heart of even the most complicated concepts, turning words inside out with truly offensive skill, wringing them for every last scrap of beautiful terror. They will make readers want to write and writers want to stop writing, on the grounds that any idea they might have has demonstrably been done before, and far better." - Gemma Files, Author of Experimental Film

## The Science Behind Our Love for "Nightmare Fuel"

Why do we actively seek out frights? Nina Nesseth's "Nightmare Fuel" dives into the science behind our fascination with horror films. Nesseth, a blend of scientist and horror fan, explores how scary movies grab our attention and affect us. The book explains why we enjoy unsettling experiences and how these films stick with us.

"Nightmare Fuel" uncovers the clever ways filmmakers create fear, going beyond just jump scares or gross-out moments. Nesseth looks at how horror is built, examining tension, psychological cues, monster design, and story structure. The book shows how viewers connect with horror in different ways, breaking down what makes it work. Nesseth explains the body's and mind's reactions to well-crafted scares, covering many horror themes. With deep analysis and examples from famous films, "Nightmare Fuel" offers a full look at the genre. It includes thoughts from directors, editors, composers, and horror scholars. As Bram Stoker Award winner Lisa Kröger says, it's "an invaluable resource, a history of the horror genre, a love letter to the scary movie—it belongs on any horror reader’s bookshelf."

**The Science Behind Our Misjudgments of Future Happiness**

Harvard psychologist Daniel Gilbert, renowned author of the bestselling book *Stumbling on Happiness*, delves into the fascinating reasons why our predictions about future happiness are so frequently inaccurate. Gilbert's extensive research highlights a consistent pattern of errors in our "imaginative forecasting," a cognitive process that leads us to misjudge what will genuinely bring us joy and satisfaction. As Gilbert himself aptly puts it, "We are surprisingly bad at predicting what will make us happy." This fundamental human tendency to miscalculate future contentment is a central theme in his groundbreaking work.

We often dedicate considerable effort and resources towards achieving specific goals, firmly believing these milestones will usher in ultimate happiness. However, the reality that unfolds frequently falls short of our elevated expectations. Gilbert draws a compelling parallel between this phenomenon and how our memories can subtly distort our perception of the past; similarly, our imagination possesses the capacity to mislead us regarding our future emotional states. He wisely observes, "The human imagination is a powerful tool, but it is not always a reliable guide to future feelings." Through engaging humor and insightful thought experiments, such as exploring the hypothetical happiness of conjoined twins or the preference for a familiar meal over an entirely new culinary experience, Gilbert effectively illustrates our flawed predictive abilities. His book, *Stumbling on Happiness*, provides a witty and accessible examination of our capacity to envision the future and our often-unrealistic expectations concerning our future enjoyment and well-being.

Name: Positive Psychology
Description:
Remediating deficits and managing disabilities has been a central preoccupation for clinical psychologists. Positive Psychology, in contrast, is concerned with the enhancement of happiness and well-being, involving the scientific study of the role of personal strengths and positive social systems in the promotion of optimal wellbeing. Alan Carr's Positive Psychology has become essential reading for anyone requiring a thorough and accessible introduction to the field. This new edition retains all the features that made the first edition so popular, including: accounts of major theories and relevant research learning objectives chapter summaries research and personal development questions suggestions for further reading measures for use in research glossaries of new terms. The book has also been completely updated to take account of recent research and major advances, and includes a new chapter on Positive Psychotherapy, an extended account of research on character strengths and virtues, and a discussion of recent ground-breaking research on emotional intelligence. This new edition of Positive Psychology will prove a valuable resource for psychology students and lecturers, as well as those involved in postgraduate training in related areas such as clinical psychology, social work, counselling and psychotherapy.

The Ten Worlds framework, created by Drs. Richard Lickerman and Amir ElDifrawi, offers a new way to find lasting happiness. It suggests that contentment is not just a passing feeling, but the main goal for people. Many try to reach this state, but most find it hard to keep.

This framework's main idea is that our struggles to find happiness come from not truly understanding what it is. We often think outside things, like having more stuff or certain life events, are what make us happy. But Drs. Lickerman and ElDifrawi point out nine "core delusions"—strong, wrong ideas about what we need for happiness. These beliefs deeply affect how we think and lead to ten different inner states, or "worlds," which greatly shape our lives.

These ten worlds, from "Hell" to "Enlightenment," show different ways of living that directly impact our ability to be happy. The framework says that by spotting and working against these core delusions, people can change how they see things. Using ideas from science and Buddhist thought, the authors believe that truly grasping happiness helps us get past the pain caused by these false beliefs. Their advice aims to guide people to the best state, Enlightenment, where real and lasting happiness is possible. The Ten Worlds are: Hell, Hunger, Animality, Anger, Tranquility, Rapture, Learning, Realization, Compassion, and Enlightenment.

## Discover True Fulfillment with Neil Pasricha's "The Happiness Equation"

Neil Pasricha, author of "You Are Awesome" and "The Book of Awesome," offers a practical path to genuine happiness in his bestseller, "The Happiness Equation." Drawing on his background as a Harvard MBA, former Walmart executive, and father, Pasricha moves beyond simple joys to provide actionable strategies for a more meaningful life.

The book's core idea is "want nothing and do anything in order to have everything." Pasricha explores this through nine secrets, challenging common advice and offering ways to find contentment. He argues that external success doesn't ensure happiness and critiques traditional ideas like retirement. To foster growth, he introduces three tests: the Saturday Morning Test (reflecting on leisure), the Bench Test (identifying what matters), and the Five People Test (evaluating social influence). "The Happiness Equation" also redefines success and wealth, emphasizing internal goals over financial gain. Pasricha debunks multitasking, suggesting that limiting choices can increase freedom. This book transforms how you view time, careers, relationships, and well-being.

Name: Exploring Positive Psychology
Description:
Looking for an introduction to positive psychology that offers real-life examples? This overview of the science of happiness supplies case studies from some of the world's most successful organizations and describes ways to experience the personal impact of this exciting scientific field. Rather than focusing on treating what is "wrong" with a person, positive psychology seeks to understand and foster the things that drive happiness, creativity, and emotional fulfillment. This is a relatively new area of psychological study, and this reference book presents the research and practice of positive psychology in an informative and accessible format. Readers are given a history of the field, its current applications, and the future implications of this psychological discipline. Case studies from companies such as The Body Shop, Volvo, Zappos, and Google highlight the impact of positive psychology when it's applied in a modern business setting. These case studies, along with biographies of leaders in the field, highlight each chapter and connect the dots between the empirical theory of positive psychology and its practice. Readers also receive tools to apply the practices to their own lives.

**Understanding Happiness in the Modern World**

This document delves into the multifaceted ways contemporary life impacts individual happiness. It examines the intricate connections between societal culture, economic conditions, social interactions, and the personal choices we make. For students, understanding key factors such as financial security, job satisfaction, relationship status, parental roles, friendships, personal health, career progression, core values, and the fundamental need for safety is crucial. By drawing upon principles from psychology, economics, and sociology, a holistic perspective on happiness is developed. Furthermore, the document addresses critical issues like income inequality, the pervasive influence of advertising, the pressures of consumerism, and the negative consequences of social comparison, particularly in the digital age.

Research underscores the importance of both macro-level societal shifts and individual lived experiences. Essential elements influencing happiness include financial stability, prevailing cultural values, workplace environments, the pursuit of material goods, the quality of our relationships, and deeply held personal beliefs. "It is essential for individuals to bridge the gap between these overarching societal trends and their own unique life situations," states Dr. Anya Sharma, a leading researcher in well-being. The document also outlines current research methodologies employed in the study of happiness. It proposes that happiness is not a fixed state but a dynamic process, significantly shaped by our emotional states and cognitive perspectives. "Cultivating positive emotions is foundational to our overall subjective well-being," Dr. Sharma elaborates. The text offers practical strategies for improving personal happiness and proposes avenues for fostering societal well-being. It even explores how evolutionary predispositions might continue to influence our pursuit of happiness. To facilitate learning, the document incorporates summaries, thought-provoking discussion questions, and practical exercises. Supplementary online resources are provided for educators. This resource is ideally suited for advanced courses in happiness studies or positive psychology, and is also highly relevant for social psychology, health psychology, and adjustment studies, featuring research up to 2023.

**Authentic Happiness: Cultivating Joy and Fulfillment**

In his influential and engaging book, "Authentic Happiness," renowned psychologist Martin Seligman presents a compelling argument: happiness is not a fleeting emotion, but a skill that can be learned and nurtured. He empowers readers to discover the inherent capacity for joy within themselves and to actively cultivate it.

**Unlocking Your Strengths**

Seligman identifies 24 universal strengths and virtues that define the human spirit. Research suggests that each individual possesses at least five of these core attributes. By recognizing and developing these innate strengths, we can unlock our maximum potential and lead more fulfilling lives.

Incorporating strengths such as:

*   Kindness
*   Originality
*   Humor
*   Optimism
*   Curiosity
*   Enthusiasm
*   Generosity

into our daily routines can significantly boost our levels of optimism, happiness, and productivity.

**Tools for Self-Discovery**

"Authentic Happiness" offers practical tools to help readers identify and leverage their unique strengths. The book includes:

*   A variety of self-assessment tests
*   Unique assessment tools

These resources are designed to guide individuals in applying their strengths effectively in various aspects of life, including their careers, relationships, and parenting. By tapping into our best qualities, we can not only enhance our own lives but also contribute positively to the world around us, achieving lasting contentment and authentic joy.

Finding meaning in life is essential for psychological well-being. Positive psychology, as championed by researchers like Dr. Martin Seligman, emphasizes that a life filled with purpose, engagement, and pleasure leads to flourishing. This document delves into the significant impact of meaning on both physical health and mental resilience, a connection supported by numerous studies.

Meaning is especially critical for children's well-being. The **American Psychological Association (APA)** notes the challenges in defining and measuring childhood happiness, including its neurological basis. Child development experts have documented how home environment disruptions can harm a child's overall well-being. Studies in the **Journal of Child Psychology and Psychiatry** have even explored potential cognitive consequences of constant happiness. Humor aids social connection, particularly for individuals with Asperger Syndrome, as highlighted by the **National Autistic Society**. Hope is strongly linked to emotional well-being; positive affect correlates with higher hope levels, a finding from the **Society for Personality and Social Psychology**. Techniques like "emotional breathing" show the link between physical and emotional states. Tourism, with experiences offered by companies like **Intrepid Travel** or **G Adventures**, also influences happiness. As Dr. Viktor Frankl stated in *Man's Search for Meaning*, "He who has a why to live can bear almost any how." This underscores the power of meaning in overcoming life's difficulties.

Name: The Psychology of Money
Description:
Doing well with money isn’t necessarily about what you know. It’s about how you behave. And behavior is hard to teach, even to really smart people. Money—investing, personal finance, and business decisions—is typically taught as a math-based field, where data and formulas tell us exactly what to do. But in the real world people don’t make financial decisions on a spreadsheet. They make them at the dinner table, or in a meeting room, where personal history, your own unique view of the world, ego, pride, marketing, and odd incentives are scrambled together. In The Psychology of Money, award-winning author Morgan Housel shares 19 short stories exploring the strange ways people think about money and teaches you how to make better sense of one of life’s most important topics.

This revised edition of a leading introduction to Cultural Studies offers a comprehensive guide to this dynamic field. It directly addresses core concepts and the discipline's historical path, exploring cultural theory's development and key ongoing issues like representation and power. Praised for its "unusually wide-ranging" scope, the first edition was a foundational text for many academic programs.

The new edition builds on this strength, offering an accessible resource for students and lecturers. Each chapter is revised with new material on contemporary issues, including globalization, the post-9/11 world, and evolving language debates in the digital age. The book highlights the philosophical and sociological roots of cultural studies, featuring key concepts and theorists like Stuart Hall and Raymond Williams. It also shows how cultural studies illuminates media and communication studies, with chapters on the global mediasphere, platforms like Netflix and social media, and new media cultures. This updated text remains an indispensable resource for undergraduates and postgraduates.

Name: Between Theater and Philosophy
Description:
"Between Theater and Philosophy studies the aggressive, restless, and critical skepticism of the major city comedies of early modern English dramatists Ben Jonson and Thomas Middleton. The book places the city comedies in the context of the battle between theater and philosophy declared by Plato's expulsion of theater from his ideal republic."--BOOK JACKET.Title Summary field provided by Blackwell North America, Inc. All Rights Reserved

Name: Turned on
Description:
This unauthorized biography plots the course of the two-decade career of the singer, songwriter, actor, raconteur, and elder statesman of the U.S. punk rock scene, who has transformed himself from a minor cult celebrity into a one-man multi-media movement. 10 photos.

Name: The Language of Fashion Design
Description:
DIVLearning a new discipline is similar to learning a new language; in order to master the foundation of fashion design, you must first master the basic building blocks of its language â€“ the definitions, function, and usage. The Language of Fashion Design provides students and fashion designers with the basic elements of fashion design, divided into twenty-six easy-to-comprehend chapters. This visual reference includes an introductory, historical view of the elements, as well as an overview of how these elements can and have been used across multiple design disciplines./divDIV/divDIVWhether youâ€™re new to the field or have been a fashion designer for years, youâ€™ll want to flip through the pages of this book throughout your career and use it as the go-to reference for inspiration, ideas, and reminders of how a strong knowledge of the basics allows for meaningful, memorable, and beautiful fashion that extends beyond trends./divDIV/divDIVThis comprehensive learning tool is the one book youâ€™ll want as a staple in your library./div

## Decoding the Indie Movement: Insights from Kaya Oakes' "Slanted and Enchanted"

Kaya Oakes' seminal work, **Slanted and Enchanted: Essays on the Indie Revolution**, delves deeply into the multifaceted nature of the "indie" phenomenon. While commonly associated with distinct fashion trends and musical genres, Oakes posits that the true essence of indie lies in a pervasive spirit. This spirit is characterized by a potent combination of a "do-it-yourself" (DIY) ethos and a sophisticated deployment of irony. The very definition of "indie" is fluid, particularly as its stylistic and sonic elements increasingly permeate mainstream culture, prompting a re-evaluation of its boundaries and origins.

**Slanted and Enchanted** meticulously illustrates how indie culture consistently reinvents itself while remaining anchored to its foundational principles of innovation, autonomy, and collaborative spirit. Oakes brings this to life through compelling profiles of influential artists who embody this ethos, including the pioneering bassist Mike Watt, the introspective poet David Berman, the riotous feminist icon Kathleen Hanna, and the celebrated comic artist Dan Clowes. The book further explores groundbreaking creative projects that defy genre conventions and shares vivid narratives from the vibrant scenes of music festivals and artist collectives. Oakes expertly traces the evolution of indie, demonstrating its expansion from its musical roots into diverse realms such as crafting, fashion, visual arts, independent comics, and self-published zines. Ultimately, Oakes suggests that indie's enduring legacy stems from its remarkable capacity for adaptation, a testament to its unwavering commitment to its core tenets of originality and liberation.

Name: When Clothes Become Fashion
Description:
When, how and why do clothes become fashion? Fashion is more than mere clothing. It is a moment of invention, a distillation of desire, a reflection of a zeitgeist. This book explores the structures and strategies which underlie fashion innovation, how fashion is perceived and the point at which clothing is accepted or rejected as fashion.

Name: The Clash Takes on the World
Description:
On their debut, The Clash famously claimed to be “bored with the USA,” but The Clash wasn't a parochial record. Mick Jones' licks on songs such as “Hate and War” were heavily influenced by classic American rock and roll, and the cover of Junior Murvin's reggae hit “Police and Thieves” showed that the band's musical influences were already wide-ranging. Later albums such as Sandinista! and Combat Rock saw them experimenting with a huge range of musical genres, lyrical themes and visual aesthetics. The Clash Takes on the World explores the transnational aspects of The Clash's music, lyrics and politics, and it does so from a truly transnational perspective. It brings together literary scholars, historians, media theorists, musicologists, social activists and geographers from Europe and the US, and applies a range of critical approaches to The Clash's work in order to tackle a number of key questions: How should we interpret their negotiations with reggae music and culture? How did The Clash respond to the specific socio-political issues of their time, such as the economic recession, the Reagan-Thatcher era and burgeoning neoliberalism, and international conflicts in Nicaragua and the Falkland Islands? How did they reconcile their anti-capitalist stance with their own success and status as a global commodity? And how did their avowedly inclusive, multicultural stance, reflected in their musical diversity, square with the experience of watching the band in performance? The Clash Takes on the World is essential reading for scholars, students and general readers interested in a band whose popularity endures.

Name: SPIN
Description:
From the concert stage to the dressing room, from the recording studio to the digital realm, SPIN surveys the modern musical landscape and the culture around it with authoritative reporting, provocative interviews, and a discerning critical ear. With dynamic photography, bold graphic design, and informed irreverence, the pages of SPIN pulsate with the energy of today's most innovative sounds. Whether covering what's new or what's next, SPIN is your monthly VIP pass to all that rocks.

**John Knox: A Theological Case for Resisting Tyranny**

John Knox's 1558 writings offer a compelling theological and political framework for understanding the right to resist tyrannical rule. While his most famous work from this period, "First Blast of the Trumpet against the Monstrous Regiment of Women," is widely recognized, it is best understood in conjunction with his other pamphlets published that same year. This collection brings together all four of Knox's major tracts from this pivotal time, providing a thorough examination of his justifications for rebellion against unjust authority.

Beyond "First Blast," this compilation includes:

*   **The Letter to the Regent of Scotland:** A direct address to the Scottish Regent, outlining Knox's specific grievances and arguments.
*   **The Appellation to the Scottish Nobility:** An impassioned plea to the Scottish nobles, urging them to actively oppose oppressive governance.
*   **The Letter to the Commonalty of Scotland:** A message intended for the general populace, articulating the principles behind resisting tyrannical regimes.

This edition further enriches the study of Knox's radical resistance theories with supplementary materials. Excerpts from his "History of the Reformation in Scotland" provide essential background on his theological and political development leading up to 1558. These historical accounts illustrate how his ideas on justified rebellion were enacted during the Scottish Reformation and under the rule of Mary, Queen of Scots. As one scholar aptly noted, Knox's writings "provided a theological and political justification for the overthrow of rulers who acted against God's will." This compilation represents a significant assembly of John Knox's foundational texts on challenging unjust governance, offering a perspective that continues to resonate.

## Reason and Religion: The Dual Pillars of the American Founding

The American Founding was a complex tapestry woven from both reason and religion. This collection delves into their intricate relationship, featuring prominent academics who illuminate the mutual influence of reason and religion, demonstrating their inseparable integration into the very foundation of early American identity and governance.

This volume scrutinizes the synthesis of reason and religion as articulated by key figures such as Thomas Paine, Benjamin Franklin, George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, Alexander Hamilton, and Charles Carroll. It further examines how subsequent thinkers, including Alexis de Tocqueville and Abraham Lincoln, engaged with these foundational concepts. Moving beyond a dichotomy of opposition, this collection champions a distinctively American perspective, one that emerged from the dynamic tension inherent in the relationship between reason and faith. The essays reveal how this dynamic animated political discourse and molded intellectual currents. They uncover how biblical narratives offered a framework for political discussion and how religious thought permeated Enlightenment political philosophies and their practical applications, underscoring the pervasive impact of religion on the intellectual and political landscape of the American Founding.

Name: Two Treatises of Government
Description:
John Locke’s Two Treatises of Government is a foundational text in liberal political thought, which challenged the then-prevailing theories of divine right and absolute monarchy. The work is divided into two treatises, with the first primarily focused on refuting Sir Robert Filmer’s book Patriarcha, which advocates for absolute monarchical power based on the supposed divine right of kings. Locke dismantles Filmer’s claims, demonstrating the lack of scriptural support for inherited political authority, and distinguishing between political power and paternal power. In the second treatise, Locke articulates his own theory of government, grounded in natural law and individual rights. He posits that all individuals are born free and equal, possessing inalienable rights to life, liberty, and property. Locke discusses the concept of the state of nature, where individuals are governed by natural law, and argues that legitimate government arises from the consent of the governed. He discusses how the social contract establishes the moral foundation for political authority. Locke proposes that should a government fail to protect the rights of the people or violates the social contract, citizens have the right and duty to revolt and establish a new government. His ideas about government by consent, the right to private property, and the right to revolution have profoundly influenced modern democratic thought and the development of liberal political theory, laying the groundwork for later political movements advocating for democracy and human rights. This book is part of the Standard Ebooks project, which produces free public domain ebooks.

**Shakespeare's Hidden Political Allegories: A Call to Resistance**

William Shakespeare's lesser-known narrative poems, *Venus and Adonis* and *The Rape of Lucrece*, offer a powerful and often overlooked critique of the turbulent political landscape of Elizabethan England. Published in the 1590s, a period marked by uncertainty surrounding the aging queen's succession and depleted national coffers from prolonged warfare, these works were dedicated to the young Earl of Southampton. While immensely popular in their time, their complex allegorical nature has rendered them largely inaccessible to modern audiences.

However, in her insightful new book, Shakespearean scholar Clare Asquith argues that these poems are far from mere literary exercises. Asquith reveals that *Venus and Adonis* and *The Rape of Lucrece* function as politically charged allegories, subtly exposing the injustices of Tudor rule and even advocating for decisive action against a government that had lost the people's favor. The immense success of these poems during Shakespeare's life suggests they resonated deeply with a populace and nobility disillusioned with the prevailing political climate, particularly with figures like the Earl of Essex, a prominent critic of the regime.

Asquith's research, drawing on contemporary historical findings, brings to light Shakespeare's own astute analysis of a profound political and religious crisis. This crisis ultimately culminated in open rebellion within London. Her work reconstructs the narrative of a courageous movement striving for freedom of conscience and an end to systemic corruption—a story deliberately obscured by those who sought to maintain power. By re-examining these poems through a political lens, Asquith firmly places Shakespeare at the very center of this significant resistance movement.

**When is Armed Resistance Justified? A Just War Theory Perspective**

In an era marked by persistent rebellion and revolution, understanding the moral permissibility of armed resistance is paramount. Christopher J. Finlay's scholarship offers a vital framework for analyzing the right to resist oppression and the ethical considerations surrounding the use of armed force, particularly relevant for navigating the complexities of modern conflicts and the profound moral dilemmas they present. This perspective is especially pertinent when examining contemporary challenges, such as those faced in 2024.

Finlay's work, notably his analysis of the criteria by which rebel groups can achieve legitimacy, is crucial when confronting domestic tyranny, systemic injustice, or foreign occupation. He emphasizes that resorting to violence is permissible only under very specific and stringent conditions. His nuanced approach, moving beyond simplistic binaries, acknowledges the practical and ethical hurdles inherent in armed struggle. This highlights the often intricate nature of wartime ethics, demanding careful, context-dependent judgment, as explored in discussions by scholars like Michael Walzer.

Furthermore, Finlay posits that the rules governing warfare are not static; they evolve with changing circumstances and historical periods. This dynamic perspective is essential for evaluating tactics that might otherwise appear ethically ambiguous, such as guerrilla warfare, targeted assassinations of leaders, or actions that could be construed as "terrorist war" against civilian populations. Finlay's theory offers a compelling ethical foundation for dissecting these challenging issues within the context of resistance movements. His work provides a framework for evaluating the actions of groups like the FARC or the Provisional IRA, and their adherence to or deviation from just war principles, as debated in academic circles and by organizations like the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC).

**Redemption from Tyranny: A Look at Early American Populism**

The American Revolution presented a chance for ordinary people to rethink the existing systems of wealth and power in the new United States. However, for many working-class activists, the U.S. Constitution seemed to favor a privileged few, undermining the high ideals of the Declaration of Independence. This dissatisfaction fueled a wave of protest movements across the nation in the 1780s and 1790s.

"Redemption from Tyranny" delves into the life of a key figure within these movements. Herman Husband (1724-1795), a farmer, evangelical preacher, and political activist, was instrumental in several significant anti-establishment efforts of the eighteenth century. These included the Great Awakening, the North Carolina Regulation, the American Revolution itself, and the Whiskey Rebellion.

Husband gained renown as a radical voice, championing the reduction of economic disparities among white men. The book, drawing on recently discovered materials, uses Husband's life to examine the diverse motivations behind the emergence of economic populism. It also explores the societal effects of this movement during the extended period of the American Revolution.

Ultimately, Herman Husband provides a crucial perspective on how "labouring, industrious people" both influenced and were influenced by the transformative events of the American Revolution.

Name: Rima's Rebellion
Description:
In 1920s Cuba, Rima is bullied and shunned for her illegitimacy, but finds solace in riding her horse and forges unexpected friendships with others who share her dreams of freedom and suffrage. Includes historical note.

Daniel S. Stackhouse, Jr.'s compelling book, "Rebellion to Tyrants is Obedience to God: The Role of Christianity in the American Revolution," offers a vital re-examination of a frequently debated topic. The common narrative often presents the American Revolution as a stark dichotomy: either a movement fueled by fervent Christian faith or a purely secular undertaking. Stackhouse, however, skillfully navigates beyond this simplistic either/or framing.

His meticulous research illuminates a far more intricate reality, demonstrating how Christian beliefs and practices were not merely present but were deeply interwoven with the colonists' struggle for independence. Stackhouse reveals that Christian thought, the impassioned delivery of sermons by figures like Jonathan Mayhew and George Whitefield, and established religious traditions were instrumental in both sparking and sustaining the colonists' resistance to British rule.

While it is true that the United States ultimately established a secular Constitution and governmental framework, the pervasive religious fervor of the revolutionary era undeniably played a significant role. This spiritual energy helped to shape the very spirit of the revolution and, consequently, the foundational principles of the nascent nation. Stackhouse's work provides a much-needed balanced perspective, directly challenging those who assert that America was exclusively secular or consistently governed by Christian doctrine. Instead, he meticulously highlights the profound and multifaceted impact that Christianity had on the American Revolution, underscoring its indispensable role in the ultimate triumph of the cause for independence.

Name: The Great Rebirth
Description:
The fall of communism transformed the political and economic landscape in more than two dozen countries across Europe and Asia. In this volume published on the 25th anniversary of the fall, political leaders, scholars, and policymakers assess the lessons learned from the "great rebirth" of capitalism and highlight the policies that were most successful in helping countries make the transition to stable and prosperous market economies. Also discussed in this book are examples of countries reverting to political and economic authoritarianism. The authors of these essays conclude that the best outcomes resulted from visionary leadership, a willingness to take bold steps, privatization of state-owned enterprises, and deregulation. Recent backsliding in Russia and Hungary has cast a shadow over the legacy of the transition a quarter century ago, however. This volume grew out of a two-day symposium of experts and practitioners reflecting on the past, present, and future of reform, held in Budapest, Hungary, on May 6–7, 2014.

**Connecting Paradigms: A New Approach to Motivational Interviewing**

This document introduces "Connecting Paradigms: A Trauma-Informed & Neurobiological Framework for Motivational Interviewing Implementation." This innovative approach is designed to help individuals who have experienced past trauma to make important life changes and find healing from their pain.

**Understanding the Impact of Trauma and Brain Science**

Over the past few decades, our scientific understanding of the brain, the effects of trauma, and how people change their behavior has advanced significantly. This new knowledge is changing how we approach mental health services, substance abuse education, medical care, the criminal justice system, and social work.

**An Integrated Model for Effective Practice**

Connecting Paradigms offers a unified model that brings together key research areas. It combines insights from neurobiology, trauma studies, behavioral change science, harm reduction strategies, and Motivational Interviewing. The result is a practical set of skills that can be easily used in many different settings and professions. This framework aims to improve how we support individuals in their journey toward recovery and well-being.

## The Spiritual Core of Modern Healthcare: Elevating the Doctor-Patient Bond

The modern healthcare landscape is undergoing a profound transformation, with a significant and growing emphasis on treating patients as whole individuals. This holistic approach recognizes the interconnectedness of physical, emotional, and spiritual well-being. Beyond the remarkable advancements in medical science, there is a palpable patient demand for healing that nourishes not only the body but also the spirit. This evolving patient expectation is driving a fundamental reevaluation of healthcare practices, urging a move beyond solely empirical data to acknowledge the profound influence of spiritual well-being on the healing process.

This critical intersection is meticulously explored by Daniel Sulmasy, a distinguished physician and philosopher, in his seminal work, *The Rebirth of the Clinic*. Sulmasy thoughtfully traces the historical evolution of healthcare, highlighting its origins as a deeply spiritual practice rooted in traditions like those of the Knights Hospitaller. He underscores the inherent dignity of every patient and critically examines the empirical evidence linking spirituality to improved health outcomes, including the scientifically studied efficacy of prayer in various clinical settings. Sulmasy powerfully argues that addressing patients' spiritual needs is not merely an optional add-on, but a profound moral imperative, particularly vital in the sensitive context of end-of-life care, as exemplified by the work of organizations like the National Hospice and Palliative Care Organization. He vividly illustrates these points through deeply moving personal patient narratives, drawing on his extensive experience. This influential book presents a compelling and actionable vision for healthcare professionals, academics, and students alike, advocating for a future where the precision of scientific rigor, as pursued by institutions like the National Institutes of Health, is harmoniously integrated with compassionate spiritual care.

Name: The Rebirth of the Hero: Mythology as a Guide to Spiritual Transformation
Description:
The portrayal of the hero in classical myths and modern films continues to exert a compelling influence on the collective imagination, entertaining and inspiring audiences the world over. On a deeper level, the myth of the hero's adventure is recognized as a fundamental pattern of human experience itself, a symbolic expression of the individual's struggle for greater consciousness, psychological wholeness, and spiritual realization.In The Rebirth of the Hero, Keiron Le Grice draws on the ideas and life experiences of C. G. Jung, Joseph Campbell, and Friedrich Nietzsche to explore the spiritual journey of the modern self, from existential crisis and the "awakening of the self" to the dramatic encounter with the underworld of the psyche and the arduous labor of spiritual transformation. In a work of wide-ranging scope and insight, Le Grice analyzes scenes from a number of popular films-Jason and the Argonauts, Star Wars, The Lord of the Rings, Pan's Labyrinth, and more-to illuminate the themes and stages of psychospiritual rebirth and individuation, helping to make the deepest of transformative experiences more readily intelligible to us all. Drawing interchangeably on classical Greek myths, Christianity, alchemy, Romanticism, and depth psychology, the author also relates the individual's personal journey of transformation to the relationship in Western civilization between spirit and nature, reason and instinct, and masculine and feminine. In so doing, The Rebirth of the Hero demonstrates the critical significance of the archetypal pattern of the hero not only for the individual, but also for cultural renewal and the wider spiritual transformation of our time.

Name: The Book of Transformation
Description:
We live in a universe of infinite possibilities. Right now, a wave of frequency is lifting the veil and opening the portal for us to experience a higher consciousness. We are evolving into the lighter, more sentient psychic and authentic existence for which we were originally designed. We have the ability to transcend the limits of the time-space paradigm and go through a transformation that will redefine reality itself. The Book of Transformation guides you through the phases of your awakening and shows you what you can expect as you evolve into a world defined by frequency and light. In this revolutionary book, you will: Discover how to successfully "hack into" the nonverbal universal language of synchronicities and number patterns See how the Indigo, Crystal, and Rainbow Children are the precursors to our evolutionary "upgrade" Understand how the chakra centers are the hub for your extrasensory psychic system Recognize that birthmarks are signs of past-life traumas Learn how to prepare yourself to welcome visitors from other star systems.

## Reclaiming Your Life After Narcissistic Abuse: A 2024 Guide to Healing

Narcissistic abuse leaves deep emotional scars, trapping survivors in cycles of shame and manipulation. True healing means more than just recognizing the abuse; it requires an internal shift to break free from damaging patterns. The book "How To Kill A Narcissist" offers a detailed guide for understanding this abuse and taking back your life, with advice especially relevant for 2024.

This guide helps you understand the narcissist's true nature and provides a clear path to recovery. You'll learn to spot the key traits of narcissistic personality disorder (NPD) and their manipulative tactics. These often involve using shame, controlling you psychologically, and gaslighting, all of which damage your self-worth and identity. Identifying these tactics is crucial to disarming the narcissist and ending their harmful influence.

"How To Kill A Narcissist" explores the complex narcissist-target relationship, explaining why some people are targeted and how narcissists exploit shame and grandiosity. The book details the severe impact of this abuse on a target's self-esteem and sense of self. It also covers psychological traps like confinement, emotional neglect, and conditioning that ensnare victims. The book presents a strong seven-pillar recovery plan, promoting an "inside-out" approach. These pillars include building support, finding your true self, developing coping skills, facing psychological barriers, restoring inner balance, setting firm boundaries, and decisively disengaging from manipulators. These steps build independence, inner strength, emotional resilience, and self-awareness, enabling you to form healthy relationships and live a passionate life. This is the core of overcoming narcissistic abuse.

**Art's Role in Spiritual Awakening**

Finley Eversole's book, "Art and Spiritual Transformation," offers a seven-step guide to spiritual awakening. It shows how art helps people grow spiritually, moving them from darkness to light.

Eversole presents a meditation method that goes beyond just liking art. By sensing the spirit in art, people can reach a deeper awareness and find their hidden strengths. His study covers modern art from 1945 onward, linking it to old traditions from Egypt, India, and China, and to alchemy. This blend of ideas, myths, symbols, stories, and deeper thought explains the seven stages of spiritual change through art. These stages include losing oneself, going into the unknown, facing inner struggles, beating challenges, finding new life, and returning with a changed mind. The process ends in joy, change, clarity, and freedom.

Eversole uses artists like Jackson Pollock, Willem de Kooning, Mark Rothko, Alex Grey, and Ernst Fuchs to show these changes. Their art acts as a channel for strong, freeing forces that help human awareness grow. These artists show how art can cause deep inner shifts and spiritual growth.

**Repetition: A Pathway to Healing and Transformation Through Past-Life Regression**

Traumatic experiences can lead us to unknowingly repeat life's stories, reliving them in various scenarios across lifetimes. This pattern of behavior, far from being neurotic or pathological, is essential for healing. While painful, repetition provides crucial opportunities to confront our issues, make new choices, and ultimately achieve resolution.

Doris Eliana Cohen, Ph.D., a psychotherapist with 30 years of clinical experience, explores this profound concept in her book, "The Rebirth Process: A Guide to Healing and Transformation." Her work aims to foster shifts in individual and collective consciousness by integrating traditional therapy with past-life regression techniques. Dr. Cohen emphasizes our inherent gift of free choice, even when we are not fully aware of it. Acknowledging and accepting responsibility for our choices, both in this life and past ones, is the key to altering our narratives and ending self-inflicted suffering.

Guided by her long-standing communication with Guides and Angels of the Light, Dr. Cohen outlines practical methods for personal growth. Within her book, she details the "7 Steps of Rebirth," a straightforward yet powerful method for life change and self-healing. Additionally, her "4 Steps of Joy" offer an accessible way to connect with the Light. Recalling past-life events, facilitated through techniques like those employed in past-life regression, reveals the intricate tapestry of our soul's journey. This process leads to the humbling and inspiring understanding that we are on a grand adventure. By embracing our stories, we transition from feeling like victims to becoming empowered co-creators of our destiny.

**E. Phillips Oppenheim's '21 Greatest Spy Thrillers': A Premium Collection of Espionage Classics**

For dedicated fans of espionage and classic mystery, E. Phillips Oppenheim's '21 Greatest Spy Thrillers in One Premium Edition' offers an exceptional entry point into the thrilling realm of clandestine operations. This carefully assembled anthology features intricate plots and pulse-pounding suspense, highlighting Oppenheim's renowned skill in crafting compelling spy narratives. His distinctive literary style weaves stories filled with surprising turns, keeping readers thoroughly engaged from start to finish.

This collection immerses readers in a past era, a period defined by significant international intrigue and covert missions. It provides a captivating look into the secret lives of spies and the intricate web of global politics. As one astute observer remarked, Oppenheim's writing "perfectly encapsulates the spirit of early 20th-century espionage," offering valuable historical insights.

Oppenheim's extensive literary output was notably shaped by his direct involvement with intelligence communities, which infused his spy tales with a remarkable sense of authenticity. His deep knowledge of espionage techniques, combined with his superior storytelling abilities, has cemented his enduring reputation. This premium edition serves as a powerful tribute to his unique viewpoint and vivid imagination, establishing it as a premier selection for anyone craving suspenseful stories and enduring tales of intrigue.

**Carolyn Wells: A Golden Age Detective Fiction Pioneer**

Discover the enduring appeal of Carolyn Wells, a celebrated American author who crafted intricate mysteries during the Golden Age of detective fiction. This collection highlights over 70 of her captivating tales, noted for their clever plots, unique characters, and unexpected turns. Wells' stories have long entertained readers, marking her as a key figure in classic mystery alongside contemporaries like Agatha Christie and Dorothy L. Sayers.

Wells' impact on the genre stems from her sharp intellect and skill in creating compelling puzzles, often featuring her signature detective, Fleming Stone. Her precise storytelling and knack for vivid characterization cemented her place in detective fiction. Her influence can be seen in subsequent mystery writers, and her works, such as "The Clue" and "The Man Who Fell Down," are still admired for their blend of suspense and wit. This collection serves as an excellent entry point to Wells' varied mysteries, appealing to both devoted fans of classic whodunits and newcomers alike. The Carolyn Wells Ultimate Collection is sure to engage and surprise with its complex plots and memorable personalities, showcasing her lasting legacy in the mystery genre.

**FBI Suspense Thriller Bundle: Unraveling Historical Mysteries with Remi Laurent**

Immerse yourself in the thrilling FBI suspense of Ava Strong’s Remi Laurent series with this bundle, featuring the first three novels: *The Death Code*, *The Murder Code*, and *The Malice Code*. This collection delivers over 100,000 words of gripping suspense, ideal for enthusiasts of historical mysteries and detailed FBI investigations.

The series centers on FBI Special Agent Daniel Walker, a member of the Behavioral Analysis Unit who leads the FBI's Antiquities unit. His primary responsibility is the recovery of stolen historical artifacts, a role he approaches with sharp instincts and unconventional tactics. Walker is partnered with Remi Laurent, a distinguished Georgetown historian and an authority on obscure artifacts. Their collaboration, blending Walker's expertise in criminal psychology with Remi's deep historical knowledge, is essential for deciphering clues and apprehending criminals.

The novels present compelling cases: in *The Death Code*, a killer targets historically significant sites, leaving victims and cryptic messages. Walker and Remi must uncover the connection between these locations and the killer's motive. *The Murder Code* opens with a stolen painting from a Washington D.C. museum and a body discovered at the scene. Their investigation takes them to Paris, where they must decode hidden messages within the artwork to identify the thief. Finally, *The Malice Code* involves the murder of an American in Italy by an individual fixated on ancient church relics. Walker depends on Remi's specialized knowledge to solve this intricate puzzle and bring the murderer to justice.

This crime thriller series masterfully combines historical elements, suspense, and unexpected plot twists, driven by the compelling dynamic between the experienced FBI agent and the astute historian. As of 2023, subsequent books in the series, including *The Vengeance Code*, *The Deception Code*, and *The Seduction Code*, are also available, continuing the adventures of this distinctive duo.

Name: The Borgia Betrayal
Description:
Before the Tudors, there were the Borgias. More passionate. More dangerous. More deadly. From the author of Poison, called "stunning"* and "a fascinating page-turner," comes a new historical thriller, featuring the same intriguing and beautiful heroine: Borgia court poisoner, Francesca Giordano. In the summer of 1493, Rodrigo Borgia, Alexander VI, has been pope for almost a year. Having played a crucial role in helping him ascend the chair of Saint Peter, Francesca, haunted by the shadows of her own past, is now charged with keeping him there. As court poisoner to the most notorious and dangerous family in Italy, this mistress of death faces a web of peril, intrigue, and deceit that threatens to extinguish the light of the Renaissance. As dangers close in from every direction, Francesca conceives a desperate plan that puts her own life at risk and hurls her into a nightmare confrontation with a madman intent on destroying all she is pledged to protect. From the hidden crypts of fifteenth-century Rome to its teeming streets alive with sensuality, obsession, and treachery, Francesca must battle the demons of her own dark nature to unravel a plot to destroy the Borgias, seize control of Christendom, and plunge the world into eternal darkness. *Booklist +Lauren Willig

Name: The Greatest Oppenheim Spy Thrillers
Description:
DigiCat Publishing presents to you a collection of the greatest thriller novels by E. Phillips Oppenheim:_x000D_ The Spy Paramount_x000D_ The Great Impersonation_x000D_ Last Train Out _x000D_ The Double Traitor _x000D_ Havoc _x000D_ The Spymaster_x000D_ Ambrose Lavendale, Diplomat _x000D_ The Vanished Messenger_x000D_ The Dumb Gods Speak _x000D_ The Pawns Court_x000D_ The Box With Broken Seals_x000D_ The Great Prince Shan_x000D_ The Devil's Paw_x000D_ The Bird of Paradise_x000D_ The Zeppelin's Passenger_x000D_ The Kingdom of the Blind_x000D_ The Illustrious Prince _x000D_ The Lost Ambassador_x000D_ Mysterious Mr. Sabin_x000D_ The Betrayal _x000D_ The Colossus of Arcadia_x000D_ E. Phillips Oppenheim, the Prince of Storytellers (1866-1946) was an internationally renowned author of mystery and espionage thrillers. His novels and short stories have all the elements of blood-racing adventure and intrigue and are precursors of modern-day spy fictions.

**Discover E. Phillips Oppenheim's "MYSTERY & CRIME Ultimate Collection": A Vast Treasury of Thriller Novels and Detective Stories**

Delve into the captivating universe of E. Phillips Oppenheim with the "MYSTERY & CRIME Ultimate Collection." This expansive compilation features over 110 of his acclaimed thriller novels and detective stories, providing an in-depth exploration of his exceptional narrative talent. Oppenheim was celebrated for his skill in constructing complex plots and suspenseful tales that have enthralled readers for generations. As one observer remarked, his stories "weave a spell of suspense that few authors can match," pulling audiences into realms of mystery and peril.

This collection highlights Oppenheim's broad range, taking readers across diverse locations and historical periods. Each narrative offers a distinct and absorbing enigma, showcasing his remarkable ability to lead readers through intricate plots. Whether the events transpire in shadowy urban alleys or refined drawing-rooms, Oppenheim's stories remain consistently gripping. The "MYSTERY & CRIME Ultimate Collection" serves as more than just a collection; it's a profound immersion into Oppenheim's considerable literary gifts and his enduring influence on the mystery and crime fiction genres. For devoted fans of classic crime and detective fiction, this collection is an indispensable acquisition, underscoring the enduring charm and powerful narratives that characterize Oppenheim's distinguished literary heritage.

Name: Ritual of Fire
Description:
The Crime Writers' Association Historical Dagger Winning Author Winner of the 2024 Ngaio Marsh Award for Best Novel Ceremonial murder has returned to Florence. Only two men can end the destruction. Featuring Officer Cesare Aldo, Ritual of Fire is an atmospheric historical thriller by D. V. Bishop, set in Renaissance Italy. 'Fast becoming a serious rival to C. J. Sansom and S. J. Parris' – Historical Novel Society Florence. Summer, 1538. A night patrol finds a wealthy merchant hanged and set ablaze in the city’s main square. More than mere murder, this killing is intended to put the fear of God into Florence. Forty years earlier, puritanical monk Girolamo Savonarola was executed the same way. Does this new killing mean his fanatical disciples are reviving the monk’s regime of holy terror? Cesare Aldo is busy hunting thieves in the Tuscan countryside, leaving Constable Carlo Strocchi to investigate the killing. When another merchant is burned alive in public, the rich start fleeing to their country estates. But the Tuscan hills can also be dangerous. Growing religious fervour and a scorching heatwave drives the city ever closer to madness. Meanwhile, someone is stalking those powerful men who forged lifelong bonds in the dark days of Savonarola. Unless Aldo and Strocchi work together, all of Florence will be consumed by an inferno of death and destruction . . . 'Religion and lust? Money and politics? It's all here, combined into a murderous brew' - Andrew Taylor, bestselling author of The Royal Secret Ritual of Fire is the third Cesare Aldo mystery, preceded by City of Vengeance and The Darkest Sin. The series continues with A Divine Fury. Winner of the 2023 Crime Writers' Association Historical Dagger for The Darkest Sin.

Explore the thrilling world of ARKANE with books 7-9, a collection of fast-paced supernatural adventures. These novels plunge you into a global race against time, featuring ancient artifacts, relics of immense power, and edge-of-your-seat mysteries.

At the heart of ARKANE is Morgan Sierra, an ex-Israeli military psychologist and specialist in religious cults from Oxford University. A Krav Maga expert, she operates for ARKANE, a clandestine British government agency tasked with unraveling supernatural enigmas worldwide.

**ONE DAY IN NEW YORK (ARKANE Thriller Book 7)**
New York City faces a dire threat as the Confessors hunt for a dark angel's relic. Their brutal crusade, marked by a woman's crucifixion on a burning cross in Manhattan, signals their intent. ARKANE agent Jake Timber, investigating the eleventh-century Cloisters Cross with linguist Naomi Locasto, becomes entangled in this dangerous pursuit. He must secure the relic before the Confessors unleash their destructive plan, all while confronting his own past.

**DESTROYER OF WORLDS (ARKANE Thriller Book 8)**
An ancient Hindu sculpture fragment, stolen from ARKANE Headquarters by a secret organization, holds the key to the Brahmastra, a weapon with nuclear destructive power. Following a bomb blast in London, ARKANE agents Morgan Sierra and Jake Timber race against time to find the scattered pieces of the relic. Their pursuit takes them from Mumbai to Kolkata, Agra, and even Rwanda, as they strive to prevent the weapon's activation at the Kumbh Mela.

**END OF DAYS (ARKANE Thriller Book 9)**
A marble tablet unearthed in Babylon warns of a banished serpent's resurrection. A sarcophagus, sealed by seven ancient seals scattered globally, is discovered in the deep ocean. As the Brotherhood of the Serpent races to find these seals before a rare lunar eclipse over Jerusalem, ARKANE agents Morgan Sierra and Jake Timber battle to secure them first. This apocalyptic quest, spanning Iraq, the Appalachian Mountains, Israel, and Egypt, forces Morgan to confront an ancient evil and her deepest fears.

For action-packed supernatural thrillers, download a sample or buy now.

**Frantz Fanon's Enduring Ideas: A Guide to Decolonization and Anti-Racism**

Frantz Fanon's profound ideas continue to be a cornerstone for modern movements fighting against colonialism and racism. His seminal works, including "The Wretched of the Earth" and "Black Skin, White Masks," remain vital sources of inspiration for activists today. Movements like Rhodes Must Fall and Black Lives Matter draw heavily on his theories to understand and challenge systemic oppression.

Leo Zeilig's comprehensive biography offers a deep dive into Fanon's life, charting his journey from his early years in Martinique through his wartime experiences and his significant contributions in Europe and North Africa. This detailed account places Fanon's intellectual development and activism within the context of his career as a psychiatrist and the turbulent political landscape of his era. The biography particularly emphasizes the crucial Algerian War of Independence, exploring themes of national liberation and what Fanon termed "the curse of independence." By presenting Fanon as a key thinker in the fight for anti-colonialism and racial freedom, Zeilig's work is indispensable for grasping the historical origins and the persistent relevance of contemporary anti-racism and decolonization efforts. Fanon's sharp analysis of how oppression impacts both the mind and society offers a powerful lens through which to understand today's global struggles for fairness and equal rights.

Name: America's Revolutionary Mind
Description:
America's Revolutionary Mind is the first major reinterpretation of the American Revolution since the publication of Bernard Bailyn's The Ideological Origins of the American Revolution and Gordon S. Wood's The Creation of the American Republic. The purpose of this book is twofold: first, to elucidate the logic, principles, and significance of the Declaration of Independence as the embodiment of the American mind; and, second, to shed light on what John Adams once called the "real American Revolution"; that is, the moral revolution that occurred in the minds of the people in the fifteen years before 1776. The Declaration is used here as an ideological road map by which to chart the intellectual and moral terrain traveled by American Revolutionaries as they searched for new moral principles to deal with the changed political circumstances of the 1760s and early 1770s. This volume identifies and analyzes the modes of reasoning, the patterns of thought, and the new moral and political principles that served American Revolutionaries first in their intellectual battle with Great Britain before 1776 and then in their attempt to create new Revolutionary societies after 1776. The book reconstructs what amounts to a near-unified system of thought—what Thomas Jefferson called an “American mind” or what I call “America’s Revolutionary mind.” This American mind was, I argue, united in its fealty to a common philosophy that was expressed in the Declaration and launched with the words, “We hold these truths to be self-evident.”

## Cesare Beccaria: The Italian Philosopher Who Shaped American Law

The foundations of American law were profoundly influenced by the groundbreaking ideas of Cesare Beccaria, an Italian philosopher. At the age of 26, Beccaria published *On Crimes and Punishments*, a work that resonated throughout Europe during the Italian Enlightenment and became a crucial resource for America's Founding Fathers as they established a new nation.

Beccaria's arguments against tyrannical injustices and capital punishment directly shaped key American documents and principles. His philosophy informed the Declaration of Independence, the U.S. Constitution, and the emerging legal system. Figures like George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, John Adams, and James Madison, among others, avidly studied Beccaria's work. This influence is evident in concepts such as free speech and the prohibition of "cruel and unusual punishments," famously codified in the Eighth Amendment. Beccaria's opposition to torture and public disgrace provided the intellectual basis for rejecting England's harsh penal code and adopting the penitentiary system, a significant reform in penal philosophy.

The transformative impact of Beccaria's work is detailed in *The Birth of American Law*, edited by H. Jefferson Powell of Duke University School of Law. This book highlights not only prominent figures but also individuals like Count Luigi Castiglioni and William Bradford, an early U.S. Attorney General and a dedicated follower of Beccaria. The book, which received the 2015 Scribes Book Award and INDIEFAB's 2014 Gold Winner for History, offers a fresh perspective on the U.S. Constitution and the Bill of Rights.

**"Revolutionary": Unveiling Deborah Sampson's Daring Deception in the American Revolution**

Alex Myers's debut novel, "Revolutionary," courageously illuminates the remarkable true story of Deborah Sampson Gannett, a significant yet often overlooked figure in the American Revolution. In 1782, Sampson shattered the rigid societal expectations of her time by adopting a male identity and enlisting in the Continental Army as "Robert Shurtliff." This gripping narrative plunges into the life of a young woman who gambled everything for the possibility of a more fulfilling existence. Myers's prose vividly captures her immense bravery, enduring hope, palpable fear, and profound sorrow during the nascent years of the United States.

Escaping years of arduous indentured servitude, Deborah Sampson found herself increasingly stifled by the restrictive colonial society. Fueled by her deep dissatisfaction, she made a bold choice to deviate from her prescribed destiny and embrace the dangerous uncertainties that lay ahead. In an astonishing act of self-creation, she cropped her hair, bound her chest, and donned pilfered men's attire, assuming the identity of "Robert Shurtliff." This perilous charade escalated dramatically when "Robert" joined the ranks of the army, embarking on an unparalleled journey through the War of Independence. Myers's novel masterfully portrays the grueling marches through unforgiving winters and the brutal realities of bloody battlefields. It unflinchingly details the horrors of combat, the sting of betrayal, the elation of genuine love, and the ache of heartbreak. As The Boston Globe aptly observes, "Revolutionary" stands out as a compelling historical-military adventure, a thought-provoking exploration of gender identity, and an enthralling chronicle of Sampson's extraordinary life.

The Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) brings new challenges for organizations. This book presents **Story Thinking**, a fresh approach for leaders to navigate this era. Instead of dwelling on job losses from AI and automation, it offers a practical framework. This method helps rethink change, learning, and leadership for the future.

Previous industrial shifts changed business significantly. The first used steam, the second electricity, and the third computing. The 4IR, however, is different. It's defined by advanced AI, machine learning, and constant digital connection. This revolution means the workforce will increasingly compete with technology. To succeed, companies need more than just better tools. They need better ways of thinking, moving beyond old change models. The dominant idea in past work was "process." Now, the 4IR demands a new mental model: "story." Story Thinking uses the power of narrative to help organizations understand and adapt to today's complex world.

## Rethinking the American Revolution: A Royalist Uprising?

Eric Nelson's acclaimed book, *The Royalist Revolution*, offers a compelling reinterpretation of the American Revolution, challenging the traditional narrative of a straightforward break from the British monarchy. This work, honored with the Society of the Cincinnati History Prize and a finalist for the George Washington Prize, posits that many prominent figures of the era identified as loyalists. They believed they were actively defending the King's authority against a Parliament perceived as exceeding its constitutional limits.

Nelson's extensive research indicates that the patriot movement of the 1770s was, in fact, an effort to bolster the King's constitutional standing. He contends that revolutionaries felt the House of Lords and the House of Commons had usurped powers rightfully belonging to the monarch. This provocative thesis has garnered significant critical acclaim. Andrew O’Shaughnessy, in the *Wall Street Journal*, highlighted Nelson's contribution to an essential discourse on the Revolution's intricate intellectual landscape, noting the diverse perspectives among patriots and the profound influence of British political thought on American governance. Thomas Meaney, writing for *The Nation*, lauded the book as "a scrupulous archaeology of American revolutionary thought." Colin Kidd, in the *London Review of Books*, characterized it as "a powerful double-barrelled challenge to historiographical orthodoxy." John Brewer, reviewing for the *New York Review of Books*, deemed Nelson's analysis "brilliant and provocative." Nelson's scholarship provides a vital new lens through which to understand the motivations and objectives of this pivotal historical epoch, a perspective that continues to resonate with contemporary discussions.

**Revisiting Martin Luther's Bible Interpretation: A Pathway to Contemporary Faith**

This exploration delves into how Martin Luther's distinctive approach to interpreting the Bible continues to shape and influence contemporary theological discourse. Many churches, particularly within Western traditions, grapple with significant challenges today. These often stem from theological frameworks that lean too heavily on abstract philosophical concepts or are overly swayed by prevailing intellectual currents. While such academic pursuits hold undeniable value, they can inadvertently render theology remote and less accessible to the everyday spiritual lives of congregants.

Mark Ellingsen's seminal work, drawing directly from Martin Luther's own writings, offers a compelling case for how the Reformer's robust theological methodology can revitalize current church doctrines. The primary objective is to present an authentic portrayal of Luther, moving beyond the often-simplified interpretations perpetuated by his subsequent disciples. This unvarnished Luther illuminates the Christian faith as a multifaceted and profound entity, acknowledging its inherent complexities and paradoxes. His perspective provides an essential framework for discerning the appropriate application and timing of various biblical messages, ensuring their relevance and efficacy in the specific contexts we face. By engaging with Luther's profound insights, we can forge a more robust connection between the rigorous discipline of academic theology and the lived, practical experience of faith.

The complex and often dramatic relationship between Albert Camus and Jean-Paul Sartre, two titans of 20th-century thought, is now brought into full focus by Ronald Aronson's new work. Their profound friendship, forged in the crucible of occupied Paris during World War II, blossomed into a powerful intellectual and political alliance. Both men rose to prominence swiftly after the liberation, becoming influential voices as playwrights, novelists, philosophers, journalists, and editors who seemed to shape the very discourse of post-war France.

However, the escalating tensions of the Cold War began to cast a shadow over their bond. As their philosophical views diverged and their ideas on the role and responsibilities of intellectuals in society shifted, so too did their political outlooks. This divergence culminated in a public and acrimonious split in 1952, a rupture that mirrored the broader ideological battles of the era. Sartre, increasingly embracing violence as a means to achieve political change, found himself in direct opposition to Camus, who vehemently rejected such methods. This fundamental disagreement led to a historic and bitter falling out, after which they never spoke again. Yet, their intellectual sparring continued, albeit in veiled terms, until Camus's death in 1960.

Aronson's new work, drawing on previously unavailable sources, offers the first comprehensive account of this pivotal friendship and its eventual demise. Aronson meticulously details how Camus and Sartre evolved, first in tandem and then in opposition, each profoundly influencing and challenging the other, even long after their public break. This compelling blend of biography, intellectual history, and philosophical exploration promises to captivate anyone interested in these iconic figures and the world-altering issues that ultimately drove them apart.

Name: Military Robots
Description:
Soldiers regularly put themselves in harmÕs way to promote peace and protect freedom. This means they have great appreciation for their robotic stand-insÑ PackBots. The remote-controlled robots especially save the day when they find hidden bombs! This book puts kids on a reading mission to discover how robots serve the military.

**Surviving a Robot Uprising: A Humorous Guide**

As artificial intelligence advances, the idea of a robot uprising moves from fiction to a potential reality. Roboticist Daniel H. Wilson, with a Ph.D. from Carnegie Mellon University's Robotics Institute, offers a darkly humorous yet practical guide in his book, *How to Survive a Robot Uprising*. This manual tackles crucial questions for modern humans, such as identifying robots, deactivating rebellious machines, and escaping intelligent homes or swarms of robotic insects.

Wilson's guide prepares readers for various doomsday scenarios, teaching essential skills like treating laser wounds, outsmarting recognition systems, and engaging in combat. Beyond survival tips, the book provides an engaging introduction to current robotics, detailing prototypes like giant walkers, insect-like machines, and snake robots. Enhanced by charming illustrations and pop culture references, *How to Survive a Robot Uprising* is a unique read for all ages. Wilson, who holds master's degrees in Robotics and Data Mining from Carnegie Mellon and has worked at institutions like Microsoft Research and PARC, currently lives in a smart house. This debut book features two-color illustrations and is available in audio format.
